pages.raw_text:
DENVER, March 8.-"Women in
Colorado and other states where
equal suffrage exists are playing
the game of follow the leader


Few Of them even read the
newspapers. Fewer think for them
selves.


"The only result Of equal suf-
frage is to swell the vote and cause
family discords. Women could do
a great deal in the interest OF good
government II they would, but the
lamentable fact remains that only
very few are willing to exert
themselves.


Il fail to see wherein womans'
suffrage is of any real benefit in
mitigating the evil Of corrupt
politics"


These are some of the views of
Rev. David Utter, an up to date
minister of the Unitarian church
of Denver. He has been making
the question of equal suffrage and
it's attendant effects on home and
state the subject of careful con-
sideration and study.


THE mass of women take "a
slight interest in political affairs"
said DR.. Utter. "At election time
the great bulk of them ask their
husbands or men friends how they
shall vote. Scores of them consult
me at every election as to how
they shall vote.


From my personal experience
in Colorado and Utah, should say
the only effects of women voting
is an increased ballot. numerically,
and a surfeit of family jars.
